title: Biomes
text: Which biomes hold the most Pokémon — computed from all 1024 species' real spawn data. Auto-generated from the spawn data of all 1024 species — this table counts how many different Pokémon can spawn in each biome group, straight from this server's datapacks. How biomes work here Cobblemon assigns spawns to biome tags (groups like "is_jungle" or "is_freezing") rather than single biomes, so one row below usually covers several vanilla and Terralith biomes at once. Rarity, time-of-day, weather, and light conditions then narrow down what actually appears — check any species in the Pokédex for its exact conditions. Terralith This pack ships Terralith world generation as a datapack, adding nearly a hundred new overworld biomes. They matter for more than scenery: 27 CobbleVerse structures generate exclusively in Terralith biomes (for example Alice in Moonlight Grove (Terralith), Dyna Tree in Sakura Grove (Terralith), Fiammetta in Forested Highlands (Terralith)…), so learning the new biomes pays off directly when structure-hunting. title: Legendary Monuments
text: 31 legendary/mythical sites across all 4 regions — 12 dedicated monuments + 19 named landmark sites. Auto-generated from every region pack's worldgen files — now covering all four regions, including named landmark sites (Sky Pillar, Spear Pillar, Bell Tower…) that previous versions of this page missed. Landmark→Pokémon associations are grounded in the pack's own radar-crafting recipes plus Pokémon canon; confirm any you're unsure of in-game. What is it? Legendary and Mythical Pokémon in CobbleVerse each guard a unique structure that generates naturally in the world, rather than only spawning via events. Spacing/Separation are vanilla structure-placement values (in chunks) — higher spacing means rarer. Watch the dimension badges: some sites are in the Nether or The End. How do I find them? Two tools, both from LumyMon : craftable radar items tuned to a specific legendary (about 30 recipes across the regions), and Cartographer treasure maps that point straight at gyms and league towers to get you into the right area. title: Mega Evolution
text: 32 custom Mega Stones craftable on this server, pulled from the Mega Showdown datapacks. Auto-generated from zamegas/recipe and mega_showdown/recipe . Species matched against this pack's own Pokédex data by name similarity — double-check any you're unsure of in-game. What is it? CobbleVerse adds Mega Evolution via the Mega Showdown mod, and goes well beyond the official Mega roster — Pokémon that never had a canon Mega form (Meganium, Greninja, Feraligatr, Emboar, Zeraora, Raichu, and more) get custom Mega Stones here, alongside official ones. Where do I find the base stones? Each recipe converts a raw lumymon: stone into the usable zamega: item — but the datapacks don't say where the raw stone drops. Likely a raid reward for that Pokémon's Mega raid boss (see the Raids page) — confirm in-game and fill in here. title: Crafting
text: Apricorns, Poké Ball tiers, specialty balls, and every other crafting system on the server. Hand-written page — lives in content/gameplay/items/crafting.html , safe from the generators. Recipes below are cross-checked against the official Cobblemon Wiki; anything server-specific is marked. Apricorns — the core catching resource Apricorns grow in seven colors (red, yellow, green, blue, pink, black, white) on apricorn trees. Right-click a fully grown apricorn to harvest one — it regrows from a bud, taking about one Minecraft day, and harvesting has a ~10% chance to also drop a plantable apricorn seed. Don't break unripe buds: they drop nothing. Bone meal speeds growth. A small farm of each color near your base pays for itself fast. Poké Ball crafting tiers Tier Recipe pattern Catch rate Basic balls (Poké, Citrine, Verdant, Azure, Roseate, Slate, Premier) 4 same-color apricorns around 1 copper ingot (red = Poké Ball, yellow = Citrine, green = Verdant, blue = Azure, pink = Roseate, black = Slate, white = Premier) 1× Great Ball Multi-color apricorn pattern + 1 iron ingot 1.5× Ultra Ball Multi-color apricorn pattern + 1 gold ingot 2× Ancient balls (Legends Arceus style) 2 apricorns + 2 T

title: LumyMon
text: 36 treasure-map trades across 4 regions — Kanto, Johto, Hoenn, Sinnoh. Original content — no external wiki documents LumyMon. Auto-generated from lumymon/trades — 36 treasure map trades across 4 regional cartographers. What is it? LumyMon adds Cartographer NPCs (one per region) who trade themed "tome" items for treasure maps pointing straight at gym leaders, legendary sites, and league towers. Where do I find it? Where each regional Cartographer NPC is physically located. How do I use it? Bring the NPC the tome item shown below (plus a vanilla map) to buy a treasure map to that destination. title: Ability Capsule
text: Swaps a Pokémon between its two regular abilities.

title: Ability Patch
text: Changes a Pokémon's ability to its Hidden Ability.

title: Ability Shield
text: Prevents the holder's ability from being changed or suppressed.

title: Absorb Bulb
text: One-use item boosting Special Attack after being hit by a Water move.

title: Air Balloon
text: Grants Ground-type immunity until popped by a hit.

title: Apricorn
text: Grows in 7 colors on Apricorn Trees — the core ingredient for every basic Poké Ball.

title: Aprijuice
text: A drink crafted from Apricorns, used in some recipes and to attract Pokémon.

title: Assault Vest
text: Boosts Special Defense but prevents using status moves.

title: Berry
text: Grow on Berry Trees — used for cooking, held-item strategies, and curing status conditions.

title: Big Malasada
text: An Alola regional snack that heals and cures status.

title: Big Root
text: Held item that boosts HP recovered from draining moves like Giga Drain.

title: Binding Band
text: Increases damage dealt by binding/trapping moves.

title: Black Augurite
text: Evolution item for Scyther → Kleavor (Legends: Arceus method).

title: Black Sludge
text: Restores HP every turn for Poison types, damages others.

title: Blunder Policy
text: Sharply boosts Speed after one of the holder's moves misses.

title: Bright Powder
text: Slightly lowers the accuracy of moves used against the holder.

title: Candied Berries
text: A sweet food item made from Berries.

title: Cell Battery
text: One-use item boosting Attack after being hit by an Electric move.

title: Chipped Pot
text: Evolves Sinistea → Polteageist (Chipped/Cracked form matters for value).

title: Choice Band
text: Boosts Attack but locks the holder into one move.

title: Choice Scarf
text: Boosts Speed but locks the holder into one move.

title: Choice Specs
text: Boosts Special Attack but locks the holder into one move.

title: Cleanse Tag
text: Reduces the rate of wild encounters while walking.

title: Clear Amulet
text: Prevents the holder's stats from being lowered by opponents.

title: Covert Cloak
text: Prevents secondary/additional effects of moves used on the holder.

title: Cracked Pot
text: Evolves Sinistea → Polteageist (Chipped/Cracked form matters for value).

title: Damp Rock
text: Extends the duration of Rain Dance.

title: Destiny Knot
text: Passes on more IVs when breeding; also links infatuation both ways in battle.

title: Dire Hit
text: Raises the user's critical hit ratio in battle.

title: Eject Button
text: Switches the holder out after it's hit.

title: Eject Pack
text: Switches the holder out after any of its stats drop.

title: Everstone
text: Prevents evolution; passes down a specific nature when bred.

title: Eviolite
text: Boosts Defense and Special Defense for Pokémon that can still evolve.

title: Evolution Stone
text: Fire/Water/Thunder/Leaf/Moon/Sun/Shiny/Dusk/Ice/Dawn Stone — interact to trigger a stone evolution.

title: Exp. Candy
text: Grants a set amount of experience points.

title: Exp. Share
text: Held item that splits earned experience with the rest of the party.

title: Expert Belt
text: Boosts moves that are super effective.

title: Feather
text: Held/consumed to permanently raise a specific stat by 1 EV point.

title: Focus Band
text: Small chance to survive a KO hit with 1 HP.

title: Focus Sash
text: Guarantees surviving a KO hit with 1 HP if at full HP.

title: Fossil
text: Restore extinct Pokémon at a Fossil Machine — see this wiki's Fossils page for real combinations.

title: Full Restore
text: Fully restores HP and cures any status condition.

title: Galarica Cuff
text: Evolves Galarian Yamask → Runerigus condition item.

title: Galarica Nuts
text: Crafts into Galarica Cuff/Wreath, used for Alolan/Galarian evolution items.

title: Grip Claw
text: Extends binding-move duration to the maximum.

title: Guard Spec
text: Prevents the user's side's stats from being lowered for 5 turns.

title: Heat Rock
text: Extends the duration of Sunny Day.

title: Hyper Training
text: Maximizes an IV on a fully-leveled Pokémon.

title: Iron Ball
text: Doubles the holder's weight and grounds Flying types.

title: King's Rock
text: Evolves Poliwhirl → Politoed and Slowpoke → Slowking when traded/used.

title: Lagging Tail
text: Makes the holder move last in its priority bracket.

title: Lava Cookie
text: A Hoenn regional snack that heals and cures status.

title: Leftovers
text: Restores a small amount of HP every turn.

title: Life Orb
text: Boosts move power at the cost of recoil damage.

title: Light Ball
text: Sharply boosts Pikachu's Attack and Special Attack.

title: Light Clay
text: Extends the duration of Reflect and Light Screen.

title: Link Cable
text: Trade-evolution item usable without an actual trade.

title: Loaded Dice
text: Improves the odds of multi-hit moves landing more hits.

title: Lucky Egg
text: Held item that boosts experience gained in battle.

title: Luminous Moss
text: One-use item boosting Special Defense after being hit by a Water move.

title: Masterpiece Teacup
text: Evolves Poltchageist → Sinistcha (rare Unremarkable Masterpiece form).

title: Max Elixir
text: Fully restores PP to all moves.

title: Max Ether
text: Fully restores PP to one move.

title: Max Revive
text: Revives a fainted Pokémon with full HP.

title: Medicinal Leek
text: A farmable crop used in several cooking recipes.

title: Mental Herb
text: Held item that snaps a Pokémon out of infatuation, Taunt, Encore, and similar effects.

title: Metal Coat
text: Evolves Onix → Steelix and Scyther → Scizor.

title: Metronome
text: Power ramps up the longer the same move is used consecutively.

title: Mirror Herb
text: Held item that copies an opponent's stat boost back onto the holder.

title: Moomoo Milk
text: Restores HP when consumed by a Pokémon.

title: Muscle Band
text: Held item that slightly boosts physical move power.

title: Old Gateau
text: A Sinnoh regional snack that heals and cures status.

title: Peat Block
text: Evolution item for Ursaring → Ursaluna in Legends: Arceus style play.

title: Pep-Up Flower
text: A farmable flower with cooking uses.

title: Poké Bait
text: Thrown to attract wild Pokémon without battling.

title: Poké Ball
text: The standard catching device — 1x catch rate, craftable from 4 red apricorns and a copper ingot.

title: Poké Cake
text: A more advanced Campfire Pot food item.

title: Poké Snack
text: Made in a Campfire Pot — attracts wild Pokémon when placed.

title: Power Herb
text: Held item letting two-turn moves like Solar Beam fire in a single turn.

title: Protective Pads
text: Protects the holder from contact-move side effects.

title: Punching Glove
text: Boosts punching move power and removes their contact flag.

title: Quick Claw
text: Occasional chance to move first regardless of Speed.

title: Rage Candy Bar
text: A Johto regional snack that heals and cures status.

title: Rare Candy
text: Instantly raises a Pokémon's level by one.

title: Red Card
text: Forces the attacker to switch out after hitting the holder.

title: Revival Herb
text: Fully heals HP and cures status, but sharply lowers Friendship.

title: Revive
text: Revives a fainted Pokémon with half HP.

title: Ring Target
text: Removes one of the holder's type immunities.

title: Rocky Helmet
text: Damages attackers that make contact with the holder.

title: Room Service
text: Lowers Speed once under Trick Room.

title: Safety Goggles
text: Protects from weather damage and powder moves.

title: Scope Lens
text: Raises the holder's critical hit ratio.

title: Shed Shell
text: Guarantees the holder can switch out or flee.

title: Shell Bell
text: Restores some HP to the holder based on damage it deals.

title: Shell Helmet
text: Cosmetic/held item related to Clamperl-line Pokémon.

title: Soothe Bell
text: Increases the rate a Pokémon gains Friendship.

title: Sticky Barb
text: Damages the holder each turn and can transfer via contact moves.

title: Superb Remedy
text: Cures status conditions and restores some HP.

title: Terrain Extender
text: Extends the duration of active terrain effects.
